message.content: Impair Defenses: Disable Cloud Logs involves adversaries disabling cloud logging capabilities to evade detection. Cloud environments typically collect and analyze audit and application logs to monitor user activities. By disabling these logs, adversaries can obscure their actions and avoid detection. This technique requires sufficient permissions, allowing adversaries to turn off logging services such as AWS CloudWatch or CloudTrail before executing further malicious activities. The potential impact includes reduced visibility into malicious actions, delayed detection, and compromised incident response efforts. Common exploitation methods involve gaining elevated permissions through credential theft or exploiting misconfigurations. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict access controls, regularly auditing permissions, enabling multi-factor authentication, and monitoring for changes in logging configurations to detect and respond to unauthorized modifications promptly.
